spec_match.rationale: Concept exercises the relevant API surface (prompt(, translate() with runtime script (~2259 chars) wired to live controls, in line with the feature summary.

interactive.rationale: Real interactive surface — 1 buttons, 2 inputs, 0 selects, 2 ranges — wired up via event listeners.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (defaults, different, languagemodel, outputs, params, prompt, same, sampling, settings, sliders, temperature, topk).

edge_cases.rationale: Some failure / fallback handling shown (2 hits), but the demo skews toward the happy path and doesn't surface most edge cases the spec calls out.

references.rationale: ChromeStatus link is present but the page has no dedicated `references` section — the self-heal contract is fragile.

design_system.rationale: All colours come via 50 CSS-variable references, no raw hex, no button background override without a colour override, and not a static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Demo at least one explicit failure or edge case
openQuestions.detail: Spec / chromestatus motivation mentions failure modes worth showing — permission denial, missing-feature, empty input, malformed payload. Add a control or scripted scenario that walks the user through one and shows the resulting error or fallback.

openQuestions.title: Wrap references in a labelled `references` section
openQuestions.detail: Per CLAUDE.md rule #3, the chromestatus link is the self-heal anchor and should sit inside a clearly-labelled `references` section so the recovery script can find it reliably.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("Adds sampling parameters to the Prompt API. These control how tokens are sampled from the model, giving developers control over the "creativeness" or "randomness" of the output. Additionally, it adds attributes to the LanguageModel instance") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://webmachinelearning.github.io/prompt-api to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~2259 chars of script across 1 button(s) and 4 other control(s). hits 4 pass / 2 partial across the six rubric criteria with no outright fails.
